name: Meditation & Breathwork App
prompt:
Create a complete single-file HTML landing page for a meditation and breathwork app called 'LUMI'. The feeling this page must create: stepping into a room where everything is quiet, slightly warm, and gently alive — the visual equivalent of a slow exhale. Nothing on this page should feel sudden or sharp.

Design language: near-white background (#FAFAF7), dusty lavender (#A89EC9) as the primary accent, warm sand (#E8D9C5) as a secondary tone, deep slate (#1C1F2E) for text. Google Fonts: 'DM Serif Display' for headlines, 'DM Sans' for body. Border-radius is generous everywhere (20px+). No hard shadows — use only diffuse multi-layer box-shadows (e.g. '0 8px 40px rgba(168,158,201,0.2)').

Sections and requirements:
(1) HERO — full viewport. Centered layout. The centerpiece is a large CSS circle (320px) with a soft lavender radial gradient that slowly expands and contracts on a 4-second CSS keyframe loop — simulating a breathing guide (inhale: scale 1.0 to 1.18 over 4s, exhale: scale 1.18 back to 1.0 over 4s). The text 'breathe in' and 'breathe out' fade-swaps inside the circle in sync with the animation using JS that toggles a class. Above the circle, a small all-caps tracking-wide label 'LUMI · BREATHWORK'. Below, a 2-line headline in DM Serif Display. Below that, two buttons: 'Begin Free' (lavender background) and 'See how it works' (text-only with an animated arrow →).

(2) SOCIAL PROOF strip — a gentle horizontal row of 5 short testimonial fragments (just one sentence each, no names) in italic DM Serif, separated by a small sand-colored dot, no borders, no cards — just floating text on the background color. The strip scrolls horizontally via a marquee CSS animation, very slowly.

(3) FEATURES — 3 cards in a row. Each card is a tall rounded rectangle (border-radius 24px) with a warm sand top half that contains a CSS illustration (simple geometric: a crescent moon for sleep, a sun spiral for morning routines, a waveform for breathing tracks), and a white bottom half with a short title and 2-line description. Cards have no border — they are distinguished purely by shadow. On hover, they translate up 6px with a smooth 0.4s ease transition.

(4) SESSION PREVIEW — a centered dark card (slate #1C1F2E, border-radius 28px, max-width 640px). It simulates the in-app experience: a current session name in large serif white, a thin lavender progress bar that animates across 8 seconds then loops, a gentle waveform visualization built from 24 CSS bars of varying heights that pulse up and down offset from each other using CSS animation-delay increments, and a row of 3 control icons (previous, play/pause, next) built as CSS circles with unicode symbols inside. The play button toggles a 'playing' class on click that changes the icon and starts the waveform pulse animation.

(5) SCIENCE section — white background, centered, a large pull quote in DM Serif italic about the science of breathing, below it 3 horizontal stat rows (e.g. '68% reduction in cortisol levels') where the number part is in large lavender and the label is in small body text, and a count-up animation triggers when scrolled into view via IntersectionObserver.

(6) PLANS — 2 tiers only (Free and Premium). Two side-by-side cards, the Premium card has a lavender gradient background. Feature lists use a soft checkmark (a CSS circle with a white tick). A monthly/annual toggle at the top, annual shows a 'Save 40%' badge in sand color.

(7) FOOTER — minimal. Three lines: the logo wordmark LUMI in DM Serif Display, a one-line tagline, and small copyright text. No columns, no links grid. Generous padding top and bottom.

Global: no jarring entrances — all IntersectionObserver animations use opacity 0→1 and translateY 20px→0 with 0.8s ease. Page background is not pure white, it is #FAFAF7 — warm off-white. Cursor is the default system cursor, unchanged.

name: Indie Vinyl Record Shop
prompt:
Create a complete single-file HTML landing page for an independent vinyl record store called 'SIDE B RECORDS'. The feeling this page must create: a warm Saturday afternoon in a beloved record shop — dusty, tactile, nostalgic, a little worn at the edges but deeply loved. The kind of place where you lose 3 hours without noticing.

Design language: warm cream (#F5EFE0) background, near-black (#1A1409) text, faded red (#C0392B) accent, warm amber (#D4820A) secondary. Google Fonts: 'Abril Fatface' for display headlines, 'IBM Plex Mono' for labels and prices, 'Lora' for body text. Textures matter here — use CSS repeating patterns (repeating-linear-gradient with very subtle lines) to give surfaces a slight grain. Intentional imperfection: slightly off-angle elements (rotate 1-2deg on some cards), uneven spacing that feels handmade.

Sections and requirements:
(1) NAV — cream background, the store name in Abril Fatface, a red underline that is 3px and slightly wavy (achieved via SVG as a background-image on the text element). Nav links in IBM Plex Mono uppercase. A 'NOW PLAYING' indicator in the nav right side: a small spinning vinyl disc (a CSS circle with concentric ring CSS borders, rotating infinitely at 2rpm) next to the text 'Miles Davis — Kind of Blue' in mono small text.

(2) HERO — full viewport. Left side (60%): a massive Abril Fatface headline 'RECORDS WORTH LOSING TIME IN' — the word 'TIME' is in faded red. Below it a paragraph in Lora. Below that, a CTA 'Browse The Stacks' and a secondary text link 'Sell your collection →'. Right side (40%): a large CSS-rendered vinyl record — a black circle with a center label (cream circle with mock text label, the Abril Fatface letter 'B' in red as a record label logo), surrounded by concentric rings drawn with CSS outline or box-shadow. The record spins slowly (360deg, 8s, linear, infinite).

(3) FEATURED SECTION 'THIS WEEK'S PICKS' — 4 album cards in a horizontal row with slight overflow (the 4th card is half-cut-off, implying scroll). Each card: a square album cover area as a CSS gradient with a unique color palette per card (warm, moody, analogous colors), a record half-sliding-out from the top of the sleeve (an absolutely positioned black semi-circle above the square), album title in Abril Fatface small, artist in Lora italic, price in IBM Plex Mono with a red dollar sign. On hover, the record slides further out by 12px using CSS transform transition.

(4) GENRE ROWS — 5 genre sections each occupying one horizontal full-width band separated by a 1px amber line: Jazz, Soul, Krautrock, Post-Punk, Ambient. Each band has the genre name in massive faded background text (opacity 0.07, font-size 120px) and 3-4 smaller album title + artist text entries in the foreground. Hovering a band darkens it slightly and reveals a 'Browse [Genre] →' link that slides in from the right.

(5) LISTENING STATION — a centered dark section (near-black background). A mock audio player built in HTML: a horizontal track bar (cream line, red playhead circle that can be dragged — implement drag with mousedown/mousemove/mouseup), track name and artist in large and small type, a time counter (MM:SS) that counts upward from 0:00 in real time using setInterval when a play button is clicked, previous/next/play buttons in cream. The section title above reads 'PREVIEW BEFORE YOU BUY' in IBM Plex Mono uppercase.

(6) ABOUT section — off-white card, a short first-person paragraph about the shop written in Lora, warmly personal, ending with the owner's name in handwriting-style italic. Next to it (right column) a small grid of 4 square 'shop photo' placeholders as warm amber-to-cream gradients.

(7) FOOTER — near-black background, cream text. Left: store address and hours in IBM Plex Mono. Center: the spinning vinyl disc from the nav, larger. Right: 'Follow the needle' with three text-only social links. A very thin amber top border.

name: Space Tourism Booking
prompt:
Create a complete single-file HTML landing page for a private space tourism company called 'APHELION'. The feeling this page must create: the specific awe of looking at Earth from orbit — vast, silent, humbling, slightly terrifying. Every scroll should feel like you are moving deeper into space.

Design language: absolute black (#000000) background throughout, pure white (#FFFFFF) text, electric blue (#0EA5E9) for data and accent, a rare use of deep violet (#4F1787) for one special element only. Google Fonts: 'Space Grotesk' for all text (weights 300 and 700 only). No warm tones anywhere. The CSS star field is built from a background: radial-gradient with multiple white dot stops at random positions.

Sections and requirements:
(1) HERO — full viewport, black background with a CSS star field (use a large radial-gradient on the ::before pseudo-element of the body with ~30 white dot stops to simulate stars). A large circle in the lower-center represents Earth from above — a deep blue-to-teal CSS radial gradient circle (500px) with white curved CSS shapes overlaid to suggest cloud formations. Above it, in tiny Space Grotesk 300 weight uppercase with wide letter-spacing: 'APHELION SPACE · PRIVATE ORBITAL EXPERIENCES'. Below the Earth circle, a large number altitude counter that counts upward from 0 to 408 (the ISS altitude in km) rapidly when the page loads, followed by 'KM ABOVE EARTH'. The main headline fades in after the counter completes: 'YOU BELONG UP HERE' in massive 300-weight type.

(2) MISSION STATS bar — a horizontal row of 4 stats on a slightly lighter black (#0A0A0A) band: '3 Orbital Paths', '11 Days Duration', '6 Passengers Per Flight', '2026 First Launch'. Each stat has its number in electric blue Space Grotesk 700, label in 300 white, and a thin blue vertical separator between stats.

(3) EXPERIENCES section — 3 full-width horizontal bands, each a different 'experience tier': Low Earth Orbit, Lunar Flyby, Deep Orbit Station. Each band takes 80vh. Left side: a large orbital diagram built from CSS — concentric ellipses (border-radius: 50%, varying sizes, electric blue, low opacity) around a small white dot (Earth), with a small colored dot on one ellipse indicating the craft position. Right side: experience name in large type, duration and altitude specs in electric blue mono, a paragraph, and a price 'From $2.4M' in blue. As the user scrolls into each band, the orbital diagram animates — the craft dot orbits one loop using a CSS keyframe that moves it along the ellipse path.

(4) THE CRAFT section — centered, a technical side-view blueprint illustration of the spacecraft built entirely from CSS: a long oval body (border-radius), rectangular solar panel wings (absolute position), nose cone (border CSS triangle), and thruster circles at the rear. Blueprint style: white/blue lines on dark background, dashed CSS border for the outline, small label arrows made of CSS lines pointing to components (Solar Arrays, Pressurized Cabin, Docking Port). No images, no SVG — pure CSS shapes and borders only.

(5) JOURNEY TIMELINE — a vertical sequence of 6 mission phases (T-6 months: Training, T-30 days: Medical Clearance, Launch Day, Orbit Achieved, Return Sequence, Splashdown), each phase on its own row. A vertical blue line runs through all phases, with a blue circle node at each phase. The phase name in white bold, description in light grey, a duration tag in electric blue. As the user scrolls, each node fills in from grey to blue using IntersectionObserver.

(6) SAFETY section — on a slightly violet-tinted dark background (the one moment of violet — #0D0010). Bold centered headline: '47 MISSIONS. 0 INCIDENTS.' Below it, 3 safety metric cards in a row with blue borders, safety stat, and a 2-line label.

(7) APPLICATION section — a centered form card on black. Heading: 'BEGIN YOUR APPLICATION'. Fields: Full Name, Email, Nationality (select), Preferred Year (radio: 2026 / 2027 / 2028). A submit button in electric blue that fills full width. On submit: the form cross-fades to a confirmation message — 'APPLICATION RECEIVED. OUR CREW SELECTION TEAM WILL CONTACT YOU.' The whole form card has a blue 1px border that glows (box-shadow 0 0 20px rgba(14,165,233,0.3)).

(8) FOOTER — absolute black, single centered row: APHELION wordmark, a thin white separator line, and four text links. A small blue pulsing dot before the text 'NEXT LAUNCH WINDOW: Q3 2026'.

name: Artisan Coffee Roaster
prompt:
Create a complete single-file HTML landing page for a specialty coffee roaster called 'ORIGIN ROAST'. The feeling this page must create: the specific ritual of a perfect morning — quiet, purposeful, sensory. The smell of coffee you cannot actually smell but can almost taste through the design. Every detail should feel considered and unhurried.

Design language: rich espresso brown (#1C0F08) background for dark sections, warm parchment (#F4EDE4) for light sections, they alternate every two sections. Rust orange (#C4581F) as the action accent, aged cream (#E8D5B7) for secondary surfaces. Google Fonts: 'Fraunces' (a quirky warm serif with optical size axis) for headlines, 'Libre Baskerville' for body text. Generous line-height (1.8 for body). Section transitions use a diagonal CSS clip-path cut between dark and light sections — not a flat horizontal line.

Sections and requirements:
(1) NAV — transparent over dark hero, solid espresso background once scrolled. Logo is a CSS-drawn coffee bean shape: two overlapping ellipses in rust, with a center crease line. Nav links in Fraunces small text. Right side: 'Our Coffees' CTA and a small bag icon (unicode shopping bag) with item count.

(2) HERO — full viewport, espresso brown background. Large centered Fraunces headline across 2 lines: 'COFFEE SOURCED FROM THE' / 'EDGE OF THE MAP' — the word 'EDGE' in rust orange. Below it, a subheadline in Libre Baskerville. Below that, the centerpiece: a large CSS-rendered coffee cup illustration. The cup is built from: a trapezoid body (using CSS clip-path polygon for the tapered shape), a handle (two stacked border-radius: 50% divs forming a D-shape), a saucer (a wide flat ellipse), and rising steam — 3 wavy CSS lines above the cup using SVG path elements with a slow upward drift animation (translateY and opacity loop). All in warm browns and creams.

(3) ORIGIN MAP section — parchment background. Section title in Fraunces. A world map representation built from CSS: a wide rectangle with subtle text labels placed absolutely for 5 origin regions (Ethiopia, Colombia, Guatemala, Sumatra, Yemen), each region marked by a rust-colored dot with a small CSS ripple animation (box-shadow pulsing outward). Below the map, a horizontal legend row explaining the dot marker. No real SVG map — just the rectangle with positioned text labels suggesting regions.

(4) COFFEE RANGE — 4 product cards on espresso background. Each card is parchment-colored, tall, with: a circular 'bag label' design at the top built in CSS (a circle with the origin country name in curved text achieved by rotating individual letter spans around the circle center using JS that sets rotation degrees), the coffee name in Fraunces, tasting notes in italic Libre Baskerville (e.g. 'Blueberry · Dark Chocolate · Cedar'), a roast level indicator (5 CSS circles in a row, some filled rust, some empty), weight and price in small caps, and an 'Add to Bag' button. On hover, the card lifts and the circular label rotates one full turn (360deg CSS transition).

(5) PROCESS section — diagonal clip-path to dark background. 4 steps in a horizontal row: Source, Select, Roast, Rest. Each step has a CSS illustration (simplified icons: a plant sprout, a magnifier circle, flame shapes, a clock). Large step numbers in faded rust behind each. A connecting dashed horizontal line runs between steps. On scroll, each step fades in sequentially using IntersectionObserver with 200ms stagger.

(6) SUBSCRIPTION section — parchment background. A centered card with espresso border. Two subscription options as large toggle-style buttons: 'Monthly' and 'Bi-Weekly' (one is selected at a time, rust background when selected). Below the toggle, frequency-specific details update based on selection (use JS to toggle a CSS class). A frequency selector of 250g / 500g / 1kg as pill buttons. A total price updates reactively. A 'Start My Subscription' button in rust.

(7) JOURNAL section — 3 blog-post preview cards in a row, dark background. Each card: a warm gradient header image (amber-to-rust), post title in Fraunces white, a one-line excerpt in light body text, a 'Read More →' text link in rust.

(8) FOOTER — espresso background, two rows. Top row: logo and a one-line brand statement. Bottom row in small Libre Baskerville: address, three nav links, copyright. A thin rust top border.

name: Brutalist Fashion Brand
prompt:
Create a complete single-file HTML landing page for an avant-garde fashion brand called 'WRNG' (pronounced 'wrong'). The feeling this page must create: deliberately uncomfortable, confrontational, impossible to ignore. This is not a page that wants to be liked — it wants to be remembered. Rules exist to be broken here, but every break is intentional.

Design language: pure white (#FFFFFF) background, pure black (#000000) text, no other colors except one: neon yellow-green (#C8FF00) used sparingly for exactly one element per section maximum. No Google Fonts CDN. Use only system fonts in deliberate collision: 'Arial Black' at weight 900 for aggression, 'Times New Roman' italic for uncomfortable elegance, 'Courier New' for confrontational mundanity. No border-radius anywhere. No gradients. No shadows. Only flat planes, thick borders (4-8px), and raw type.

Sections and requirements:
(1) NAV — white, the brand name 'WRNG' in 48px Arial Black, tracked out to full container width using letter-spacing or flexbox justify. Right side: 3 nav items in Times New Roman italic. A thick 4px black bottom border. When scrolled past hero: nav background inverts to black, text inverts to white. No smooth transition — it snaps instantly.

(2) HERO — full viewport. The word 'WRONG' in massive Arial Black (font-size: clamp(80px, 18vw, 200px)) is printed in large black type centered on white — but the letter 'O' in 'WRONG' is replaced by a neon green circle (a CSS div with background #C8FF00, perfectly circular, displayed inline-block). Below the headline, a thin Times New Roman italic subheading in small text: 'collections for those who know better'. Below that, a single CTA: 'ENTER' in Courier New, no button styling — just underlined text. On hover, the underline color changes to neon green. A large black border box wraps the entire hero content area.

(3) MANIFESTO section — full-width black background, white text. A dense block of short-sentence manifesto text in Times New Roman italic, 20px, leading 2.0. Each sentence is on its own line. The sentences are confrontational but stylish (e.g. 'Comfort is a design failure.' / 'We do not do neutrals.' / 'If it fits in, it fails.'). One sentence — choose it — is set in neon green. No animations. Just the text, centered, maximum width 600px, on black.

(4) COLLECTION grid — white background. A CSS Grid of 6 product tiles. Layout is deliberately broken: use explicit grid-column and grid-row placement to create irregular gaps between tiles — some tiles are wider, one tile is just a black square with white text 'SS25'. Product tiles have: a black border 4px, a tall rectangle gradient (black to dark grey) as the product image area, the product name in Arial Black underneath, the price in Courier New, and an 'ADD' button in a flat black box with white Courier New text. On hover: the entire tile inverts (black background, white border, image area inverts using CSS filter: invert(1)). No easing — instant snap on hover.

(5) EDITORIAL STRIP — a full-width, 70vh section. A single large editorial 'photo' placeholder: a black rectangle the full width and height, with white Arial Black text overlaid — the text is a single word 'TENSION' at approximately 20vw font-size, positioned bottom-left. A thick neon green horizontal line (4px, full width) divides this section from the next.

(6) PROCESS section — 3 columns on white, each column separated by a 4px black vertical border. Column headers in Times New Roman italic 28px: 'Conceptual Rot', 'Structural Failure', 'Commercial Mistake'. Body text in Courier New 13px. Each column has 3-4 short sentences. At the top of one column (chosen deliberately at random-seeming position) is a neon green asterisk (*) in 60px Arial Black.

(7) ABOUT section — asymmetric layout. Left 70%: a large Arial Black question in two lines — 'WHAT IS / WRNG?' — followed by a short paragraph in Times New Roman italic answering it obliquely. Right 30%: a black vertical band with white vertically-rotated Courier New text reading 'EST. 2019 · LONDON · UNCOMPROMISING'.

(8) FOOTER — black background. First row: 'WRNG' in white Arial Black 64px, full width with tracked spacing. Second row: three columns in white Courier New 11px — left: 'ALL PIECES FINAL SALE', center: 'LONDON · PARIS · TOKYO', right: copyright. One element is neon green: the copyright symbol ©.
